description:
You are a satellite operations engineer at a commercial LEO constellation operator. You have received a Conjunction Data Message (CDM) at ~/Desktop/cdm_comms_leo_7.txt warning of a predicted close approach between your active satellite COMMS-LEO-7 and a FENGYUN-1C debris fragment. Perform a conjunction assessment by creating a GMAT script that models both objects using the orbital parameters from the CDM, including Earth gravity and atmospheric drag. Propagate the spacecraft for 72 hours to determine the Time of Closest Approach (TCA) and the minimum miss distance. Save the GMAT script to ~/Documents/missions/conjunction_assessment.script. Write a conjunction assessment report to ~/GMAT_output/conjunction_assessment.txt with the following fields:

TCA: <value>
MISS_DISTANCE_KM: <value>
RECOMMENDATION: <MANEUVER_REQUIRED if miss distance < 1.0 km, otherwise MONITOR or NO_ACTION>
AVOIDANCE_DV_MS: <value if maneuver required, 0 otherwise>

Login: username=ga, password=password123
